title: Advanced Vision Applications Capstone
concept: Phase 10 Capstone: We have journeyed from face recognition through OCR, style transfer, super-resolution, tracking, retrieval, anomaly detection, depth estimation, and 3D vision — a complete toolkit of specialized real-world computer vision applications.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the risk of shipping a defect detector straight from a strong research benchmark score to a factory floor with no further testing?

options.text: The research test set may not reflect real-world edge cases, latency constraints, or monitoring needs of actual deployment
There is no risk at all in this approach
Research and production accuracy are always mathematically identical

exercises.explain: Yes! Benchmark scores alone don't guarantee production readiness.

cards.title: A complete specialist's toolkit
cards.body: Thirteen units ago, we asked a simple question: is there a face in this photo? Since then, we've built an entire toolkit of specialized vision applications, each answering a different real-world question, all standing on the CNN foundations from earlier phases.

cards.title: Phase 10 Full Recap
cards.body: We covered: face detection & recognition, pose estimation, OCR, image captioning, neural style transfer, super-resolution, multi-object tracking, image retrieval, anomaly & defect detection, depth estimation, and 3D vision (point clouds & NeRFs) — thirteen distinct specialized applications (Units 163-174), plus this capstone's lessons on choosing, combining, and productionizing them.

cards.title: A world atlas of computer vision applications
cards.body: Earlier phases taught you to read a map (the CNN fundamentals). Phase 10 was like touring an entire atlas of specialized regions — each with its own terrain, tools, and challenges — from the 'face recognition' region to the '3D vision' region, all reachable using the map-reading skills you already built.

cards.title: A modern smartphone camera app
cards.body: Your phone's camera app likely uses several Phase 10 techniques at once: face detection for autofocus, depth estimation for portrait mode, super-resolution for digital zoom, and OCR for scanning documents — a single consumer product built from many specialized tools you've now studied.

cards.title: Mastering applications isn't the finish line — it's a bridge
cards.body: It's tempting to think that after covering this many real-world applications, the journey is complete. In reality, all of Phase 10's techniques still rely on CNN backbones — the next frontier is a fundamentally different architecture family (Transformers) that is reshaping how vision models are built.

right.text:
Style transfer & super-resolution
Tracking, retrieval, anomaly detection
Depth estimation & 3D vision

exercises.prompt: Which of these best summarizes what Phase 10: Advanced Vision Applications covered?

options.text: A wide range of specialized, real-world CNN-based applications, from face recognition through 3D vision
Only the mathematical foundations of backpropagation
Exclusively audio and speech processing techniques

exercises.explain: Correct! Phase 10 built specialized real-world applications on top of CNN foundations.

exercises.prompt: True or false: All of Phase 10's specialized applications were built on top of CNN architecture and training foundations from earlier phases.
exercises.explain: True. Every application in this phase relied on the CNN toolkit — convolutions, architectures, and training practices — established earlier in the curriculum.

exercises.sentence: A modern smartphone camera app likely combines several Phase 10 techniques like face detection, depth estimation, and ___ for zoom.
exercises.explain: Super-resolution sharpens digital zoom, working alongside other Phase 10 techniques in a single app.

exercises.prompt: Why is it accurate to say Phase 10 was a 'bridge' rather than a final destination in the curriculum?

options.text: All Phase 10 techniques relied on CNN backbones, and the next phase introduces a fundamentally different architecture family (Transformers) reshaping vision
Phase 10 represents the absolute final and most advanced topic in all of computer vision
Nothing comes after Phase 10 in the field of computer vision

exercises.explain: Correct! Phase 10 sets up the transition into an entirely new architectural paradigm.

exercises.prompt: Phase 10: Advanced Vision Applications is complete. What comes next in our Computer Vision curriculum?

options.text: Phase 10: Advanced Vision Applications is complete. Next up: Vision Transformers & Modern Approaches.
Restarting the curriculum from Logic Gates and the Perceptron
Skipping directly to model deployment without covering Vision Transformers
Deleting the entire Computer Vision curriculum and starting a new subject

exercises.explain: Phase 10: Advanced Vision Applications is complete. Next up: Vision Transformers & Modern Approaches. You've built a complete toolkit spanning faces, text, art, tracking, search, anomalies, depth, and 3D — now let's see how Transformers are reshaping computer vision itself!
